chunk_summary: This section discusses data hygiene, record retention, and communication channel separation in Indian tele-nutrition, emphasizing secure handling of sensitive child health data, explicit consent, and compliance with telemedicine guidelines for data retention and operational rules.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of separating communication channels for tele-nutrition services.
Learn best practices for data hygiene and record retention in tele-nutrition, including compliance with Indian telemedicine guidelines.
Identify operational rules for handling sensitive child health data and mitigating risks like data breaches.
Explore strategies for implementing a digital hygiene policy in tele-nutrition clinics.
key_facts: Sensitive child health data includes growth measures, photos, feeding videos, and lab reports.
Telemedicine guidelines in India require maintaining case records and digital trails for at least three years from the last consultation.
Data minimization and purpose-limited retention are emphasized for child data protection.
Operational rules should include a 'no media on personal gallery' policy and role-based access for team members.
Explicit consent is required when using common messaging apps for sensitive data sharing.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Telemedicine guidance requires maintaining case records and a digital trail (phone logs, emails, chat/text, video interaction logs) for at least three years from the last consultation.
document_key_topics: Record retention guidelines for child health data
